title: Attack input rhythm
principles: Attack rhythm describes how player input initiates, prepares, releases, and repeats attack events; it does not describe damage delivery, damage instances, or target coverage.
Trigger type, repetition behavior, charge preparation, press-release behavior, and staged activation must remain separate because one attack mode may combine several of these properties.
Fire rate must be interpreted within the complete timing model and must not be treated as guaranteed practical attack frequency.
Timing within an attack sequence and timing between complete attack cycles must remain distinct.
Charge, spool-up, Duplex, and staged activation require explicit mechanical or timing evidence and must not be inferred from damage values or generic labels.
Each attack mode must preserve its own input and timing profile.

automatic: Maintaining the attack input intrinsically repeats discrete attack events up to the mode's permitted cadence.
Automatic repetition does not establish continuous damage application or uninterrupted target contact.
semi_automatic: The mode produces one discrete attack cycle per permitted activation under its base trigger behavior.
Fire rate represents the maximum permitted cadence rather than guaranteed practical cadence.
burst: One activation initiates a defined sequence of multiple attack events.
Burst count and intra-burst timing describe the sequence, while burst cycle timing determines when another sequence may begin.
charge: The mode includes an explicit preparation stage before release or completion of the attack.
Charge time quantifies the preparation interval only when supplied for the evaluated mode.
duplex: Pressing and releasing the input produce mechanically distinct attack events.
The timing between those events may depend on input duration and must not be treated as a fixed burst interval without explicit evidence.
staged_activation: The attack sequence requires a distinct deployment or creation stage followed by another explicit activation action or condition.
The stages must remain separately traceable and must not be reduced to ordinary semi-automatic or burst behavior.
undetermined: The supplied trigger and timing data do not define how attack events are initiated or repeated with sufficient confidence.

automatic_repetition_with_spool_up: Separate initial repetition rate, acceleration behavior, and fully accelerated cadence when explicit spool-up data are supplied.
Do not infer spool-up from automatic fire alone.
manual_trigger_continuous_input_enabled: Input accessibility may repeatedly activate Semi, Burst, or Charge trigger modes while the input is maintained.
Preserve the weapon's underlying trigger sequence and timing model rather than reclassifying it as intrinsically automatic.
combined_input_properties: Preserve all supported input properties when a mode combines charge, automatic repetition, press-release events, burst sequencing, or staged activation.
Do not force a mechanically composite mode into one mutually exclusive label.
multi_mode_weapon: Preserve separate rhythm classifications and timing fields for every attack mode.

exceptions: Do not infer practical attack frequency from fire rate alone.
Do not classify maintained input as beam delivery, continuous damage application, or guaranteed contact.
Do not infer charge timing, burst structure, Duplex behavior, staged activation, or spool-up from an unsupported label.
Do not count burst attacks as simultaneous damage instances within one attack event.
Do not merge distinct attack modes into one rhythm profile.
